procurement_settlement_intro_p1: Every signed contract published on the central procurement register (АОП) is pinned to the buyer's headquarters. We exclude central ministries, state agencies and nationally-operating state companies — their Sofia HQ tells you nothing about where the contract was spent — and aggregate them separately under "National procurement".
procurement_settlement_truncated_msg: Showing the first 500 settlements — refine the search to narrow.
procurement_settlement_footnote: Buyer HQ is the location proxy. Schools, hospitals, municipalities, universities, regional government offices, forestry districts and local utilities are pinned to their HQ. National procurement (ministries, central agencies, national state companies) is rolled up separately.
procurement_settlement_not_found_body: This settlement has no local-tier procurement on record. The dataset only covers contracts whose buyer headquarters resolve to a settlement in our catalog; small villages and inactive buyers may simply be missing.
procurement_settlement_detail_footnote: Buyer HQ is the location proxy. Central ministries and national state companies based in Sofia procure nationally — they are not shown on this page; see the "national procurement" rollup on the landing page.
procurement_settlement_more_buyers: More buyers on the detail page
about_procurement_by_settlement_note: The "procurement by settlement" view (/procurement/by-settlement) pins each contract to the buyer's headquarters. The classifier excludes central ministries, state agencies and nationally-operating state companies (Sofia HQ, national footprint) — they roll up into a separate "national procurement" card. The buyer-HQ → EKATTE resolver is postal-primary against the canonical NSI settlement catalog (5,267 entries with postal codes); 99.9% of buyers in the 2026 sample resolve to a single settlement. The tier classifier uses name heuristics plus a curated EIK override table for the long tail.
risk_flag_mp_connected_long: Contractor is connected to an MP
risk_flag_mp_connected_hint: An MP appears as a declared officer or owner of this company.
risk_flag_amendment_hint: This row revises an earlier contract — common vehicle for value inflation outside the original procedure.
awarder_not_found_desc: No procurement-contract data is recorded for this awarder EIK. The buyer may not have published contracts during the ingested period, or the EIK is incorrect.
awarder_mp_section: MP-tied contractors paid by this awarder
contract_source_hint_eop: Procurement record on the public registry (CAIS ЕОП):
contract_source_hint_legacy: Legacy contract — no direct record link is available; see the dataset:
company_top_awarders_subtitle: State buyers that paid this company.
awarder_top_contractors_subtitle: Companies ranked by total amount received from this awarder.
budget_section_execution_subtitle: How the budget tracks through the year
budget_section_composition_subtitle: Where money comes from and where it goes
personnel_section_subtitle: How many people work for the state and how much they're paid
personnel_doklad_source: Report on the State of the Administration
personnel_nsi_caveat: End-of-December {{year}} headcount per NSI labor stats. Excludes MoI + MoD.
personnel_ministries_caveat: Coverage: ~30% of total first-level FY2024 expenditure (€2.7B of €8.9B). Includes only ministries whose programme-budget execution report has been ingested. MoD redacts the staffing numbers (classified); MoI and MoF FY2024 are Cloudflare-challenged (MoF FY2023 was backfilled via the Internet Archive). The remaining ~70% — including Interior, Education, Regional Development, Transport, Justice, Culture, Foreign Affairs — don't publish their programme-budget execution report at a stable URL.
personnel_drilldown_coverage: Coverage: {{covered}} of {{total}} ({{pct}}%) of the КФП Personnel line. Remainder = MOD (classified), MoI, MoF, and ministries not yet ingested.
municipalities_drilldown_coverage: State→municipal transfer envelope (Art. 53): {{covered}} of {{total}} ({{pct}}%) of the КФП Municipalities line. Remainder = municipal own revenue + non-Art. 53 transfers.
municipalities_drilldown_caveat: Article 53 of the State Budget Law sets out the annual envelope each община receives from the central budget. The five sub-categories are mutually exclusive. The КФП line additionally captures own revenue.
municipalities_by_oblast: By oblast (count = number of municipalities in oblast)
municipal_transfers_tile_intro: What this oblast's municipalities receive from the central budget. Article 53 of the State Budget Law sets the envelope split into five categories.
municipal_transfers_tile_caveat: Planned figures from Article 53 of the State Budget Law. Actual execution may differ slightly. Excludes municipal own revenue + non-Art. 53 transfers.
municipality_transfers_tile_intro: What this municipality receives from the central budget under Article 53 of the State Budget Law, split into five categories.
my_area_municipal_budget_coverage: {{n}} of 265 municipalities publish a machine-readable execution report
funds_status_tile_caveat: Filled segments show actually-paid amounts; faded extensions show the contracted envelope. Completed contracts typically close near 100% paid; in-progress and signed contracts carry the remaining absorption gap.
funds_programs_tile_caveat: Badge: disbursement rate. Green ≥80%, amber 40-80%, rose <40%. Programmes with very low disbursement either started late (2021-2027 cycle) or stalled.
funds_geo_tile_subtitle: {{pct}}% mapped to a settlement or municipality
funds_geo_tile_caveat: ИСУН's Местонахождение field is resolved against the EKATTE settlement index and municipality list. Region / national / unresolved buckets can't be attributed to a specific place — they show on /funds but not on settlement or municipality pages.
funds_map_subtitle: {{n}} municipalities · click for the per-place page
funds_map_caveat: Sofia districts share a single Stolichna aggregate. Per-capita uses Census 2021 population. Municipalities with zero contracts appear in light grey.
funds_program_source_hint: Source: ИСУН 2020 Project register. All amounts in EUR. Top lists capped at 20 (beneficiaries, contracts) or 10 (municipalities) — the full corpus lives in the by-program shard.
funds_contract_source_hint: Source: ИСУН 2020 Project register. One per-contract record fetched on demand (~1 KB).
sofia_capital_tile_intro: Per-object list of capital investments from Sofia's adopted Capital Programme — construction, repairs, energy-efficiency works and acquisition of fixed assets in this district.
sofia_capital_tile_caveat: Source: Annex №3 to the Sofia city budget bill. Figures are planned; actual execution is reported at year-end.
plovdiv_capital_tile_intro: Per-object list of capital investments from the Plovdiv municipal budget, broken down across the city's 6 districts.
plovdiv_capital_tile_caveat: Source: Plovdiv municipal-budget capital-expenditure schedule. Figures are planned; actual execution is reported at year-end. About 78% of objects are city-wide municipal-administration items with no district tag.
varna_capital_tile_intro: Per-object list of capital investments from Appendix №4 of the Varna municipal budget, broken down across the city's 5 districts. Source is a scanned PDF; extraction goes through Gemini Vision OCR.
varna_capital_tile_caveat: Source: Appendix №4 — Capital expenditure schedule attached to the Varna draft budget (scanned PDF). Figures are OCR-extracted; minor recognition errors are possible. Shows the sum of itemised objects.
burgas_capital_tile_intro: Per-object list of capital investments from the Burgas municipal budget, grouped by funding source.
burgas_capital_tile_caveat: Source: Burgas municipal-budget capital-expenditure schedule. Figures are planned; actual execution is reported at year-end. About 86% of objects are city-wide with no sub-location specified.
stara_zagora_capital_tile_intro: Per-object list of capital investments from the Stara Zagora municipal budget (Appendix №4 to the council's budget decision).
stara_zagora_capital_tile_caveat: Source: Appendix №4 to the Stara Zagora draft budget. Figures are planned; actual execution is reported at year-end. Shows the sum of itemised line-item objects. The published "КАПИТАЛОВИ РАЗХОДИ - ОБЩО" figure in Appendix №4 also includes city-wide commitments and paragraph-level rollups that don't decompose to individual objects.
ruse_capital_tile_intro: Per-object list of capital investments from the Ruse municipal budget (year-end revised plan + execution). Each village kmetstvo (Басарбово, Николово, …) has its own sheet in the source workbook, so per-village attribution is via workbook structure rather than free-text matching.
ruse_capital_tile_caveat: Source: annual XLSX from obshtinaruse.bg. Shows the revised plan for the year — actual execution may differ.
muni_exec_tile_intro: Plan vs. actual for the full fiscal year, from the municipality's ЕБК cash-execution report.
muni_exec_tile_intro_partial: Plan vs. actual for {{period}} (partial year), from the municipality's ЕБК cash-execution report.
muni_exec_plan_actual_note: Large figure = actual (отчет); small figure = revised plan; the % is execution against plan. Own revenue (local taxes, fees, property income) funds only part of spending — the rest is state transfers and carry-over, which is why expenditure exceeds own revenue.
pleven_capital_tile_intro: Itemised list of capital projects from Annexes №4 and №10А to the Pleven município budget. The PDF is technically text-extractable but the layout is fragmented — extraction goes through OCR with Gemini Vision for clean row recognition.
pleven_capital_tile_caveat: Source: Annexes №4 + №10А to the Pleven município budget. Sum of the itemised projects matches the published ВСИЧКО totals exactly (7.59M + 11.00M BGN).
sliven_capital_tile_intro: Itemised list of capital projects from the Sliven município capital expenditure financing schedule (initial plan). The source is a scanned PDF from mun.sliven.bg — extraction goes through Gemini Vision OCR.
sliven_capital_tile_caveat: Source: capital expenditure financing schedule of Sliven município (scanned PDF). Values are OCR-extracted; minor recognition errors possible. Shows the sum of the itemised projects.
dobrich_capital_tile_intro: Itemised list of capital projects from the Dobrich-grad capital expenditure programme (latest actualisation). Scraped directly from the HTML table on dobrich.bg.
dobrich_capital_tile_caveat: Source: Dobrich-grad capital expenditure programme (HTML table on dobrich.bg). Shows the sum of itemised projects from the adopted actualisation.
asenovgrad_capital_tile_intro: Itemised capital projects from the Asenovgrad município capital expenditure schedule. Born-digital PDF on asenovgrad.bg — extraction goes through Gemini Vision OCR for robustness.
asenovgrad_capital_tile_caveat: Source: Asenovgrad município capital expenditure financing schedule. Shows the sum of itemised projects.
shumen_capital_tile_intro: Itemised capital projects from Annex №6 to the Shumen município budget. Born-digital PDF — extraction goes through Gemini Vision OCR.
shumen_capital_tile_caveat: Source: Annex №6 — Capital expenditure financing plan (shumen.bg). Shows the sum of itemised projects.
vidin_capital_tile_intro: Itemised 2023 capital expenditure execution from the year-end report by Vidin município. Parsed from the .doc inside the year-end RAR on vidin.bg.
vidin_capital_tile_caveat: Source: Отчет за капиталови разходи 2023 г. (vidin.bg). Shows the sum of itemised projects.
veliko_tarnovo_capital_tile_intro: Per-project capital list for 2025 from Annex 15 of Veliko Tarnovo's municipal budget. Extracted directly from the XLSX — no OCR.
veliko_tarnovo_capital_tile_caveat: Source: Annex 15 — Investment programme 2025 (veliko-tarnovo.bg). Shows the sum of itemised projects.
pernik_capital_tile_intro: Per-project capital list for 2026 from the municipality of Pernik. Extracted directly from the XLS — no OCR. Figures are already in EUR (post-euro adoption).
pernik_capital_tile_caveat: Source: 2026 named list of capital expenditures (pernik.bg). Shows the sum of itemised projects.
haskovo_capital_tile_intro: Per-project capital list for 2024 from the municipality of Haskovo (Annex №7). 19-page landscape PDF processed with Gemini Vision OCR for robust multi-line description joining.
haskovo_capital_tile_caveat: Source: Annex №7 — 2024 capital expenditure financing plan (haskovo.bg). Shows the sum of itemised projects.

gabrovo_capital_tile_caveat: Source: Annex №5 — 2025 investment programme (gabrovo.bg). Shows the sum of itemised projects.
yambol_capital_tile_intro: Per-project capital list from Annex 4/5 of the Yambol municipal budget. The file is packaged inside an annual RAR/ZIP archive on yambol.bg. Parser uses Gemini Vision OCR.
yambol_capital_tile_caveat: Source: Annex 4/5 — Capital expenditure financing plan (yambol.bg). Yambol is a single-settlement município — no per-village breakdown.
kardzhali_capital_tile_intro: Per-project capital list from the municipal financing plan of Kardzhali. The PDF URL was discovered via Google indexing. Parser uses Gemini Vision OCR.

lovech_capital_tile_intro: Per-project capital list from "Budget and capital expenditures of Lovech municipality". The PDF URL was discovered via Google indexing. OCR via Gemini Vision (per-project amounts are approximate — the headline uses the published recap from the council decision).

ipop_tile_intro: Per-project payment status for МРРБ's Инвестиционна програма за общински проекти (Ministry of Regional Development's municipal investment grants). Same OP-YY.NNN-NNNN IDs as the State Budget Law's Annex III, but with paid / submitted / awaiting amounts.
ipop_tile_caveat: Source: ipop.mrrb.bg CSV export. Daily refresh. Stalled-project flag = agreement ≥ €100k AND paid < 5% of agreement.
noi_drilldown_coverage: NOI gross expenditure {{gross}} vs. КФП net Social-security funds line {{net}}. The gap is the state subsidy + inter-system consolidation. Drilldown sums the three NOI funds (DOO, Teachers Pension, Bankruptcy Receivables).
noi_drilldown_caveat: Source: NOI per-fund B1 monthly cash-execution reports. Cut-off date shown next to year. Pension-type breakdown (old-age / disability / survivors) requires the annual yearbook — coming in a later release.
noi_pensions_by_type_caption: Source: annual NOI pension yearbook (Table 6.3). Sum reconciles to the КФП Pensions total within ~0.5% — the yearbook excludes mid-year inter-fund transfers and a handful of edge categories aggregated under "Other", which together account for the small residual.
capital_drilldown_coverage: Municipal investment program (Чл. 113 III): {{program}} across {{projectCount}} projects. Larger than the КФП Capital expenditure line ({{kfp}}) because that figure is net of intra-system transfers. Excludes ministry direct capex.
capital_drilldown_caveat: Source: Приложение № 3 към чл. 113 of the State Budget Law (DV PDF annex). Categories assigned by regex on project names — best-effort, not authoritative.
noi_tile_intro: How NOI spends its annual envelope across pensions, short-term benefits (sickness, maternity, unemployment), and operations.
noi_tile_gross_caption: NOI gross expenditure (DOO + Teachers Pension + Bankruptcy Receivables)
investment_tile_intro: Per-project capital allocations to municipalities under the State Budget Law's investment program (Приложение III). Classified by project type via regex on the project name.
cabinet_fiscal_footprint_intro: Capital investment program + social-security spending across every fiscal year that overlapped this cabinet's tenure. These envelopes were set by the prevailing State Budget Law — the cabinet that adopted the law and the one that executed it may differ.
cabinet_fiscal_footprint_caveat: Sums data from data/budget/investment_program/ (Приложение III to the State Budget Law) and data/budget/noi/ (NOI B1 monthly cash-execution reports). Coverage = fiscal years for which an ingest exists.
personnel_ministry_source_caveat: Source: Program Budget Execution Report — Personnel line and Staffing row. Average cost includes employer social-security contributions.

budget_trend_caption_projected: Cumulative execution within each fiscal year — figures reset each January. Dashed lines extend the in-progress year through December using the prior year's seasonal pattern; the December endpoint matches the headline projection.
budget_flow_intro: Where the money comes from and where it goes. The two diagrams meet at the balance bridge — when spending exceeds revenue, the hatched wedge marks the gap closed by financing.
budget_flow_deficit_hint: Spending exceeds revenue — the gap is closed by financing (borrowing or reserves).
budget_flow_surplus_hint: Revenue exceeds spending — the surplus reduces debt or funds reserves.
budget_flow_source_hint: Built from the КФП monthly snapshot. Subtotal hierarchy is reconstructed from the source's flat label column.
budget_functional_subtitle: General government expenditure by function, fiscal year
budget_functional_caption: Source: Eurostat gov_10a_exp (general government, sector S13). Includes state budget, social-security funds (NSSI/NHIF) and municipal budgets — broader than the state-budget execution feed.
budget_tax_bill_above_cap_hint: Above the МОД cap each extra euro is taxed at only 10% — the effective rate falls as the salary rises.
budget_tax_bill_include_employer: Include employer contributions in the breakdown below
budget_tax_bill_vat_note: 20% VAT embedded in everyday spending, assuming {{pct}} of net income goes to standard-rated goods.
budget_tax_bill_pension_into: Paid into the pension system: {{total}}{{per}} (you {{you}} + employer {{employer}}).
budget_tax_bill_pension_into_self: Paid into the pension system: {{total}}{{per}} (you remit the full amount).
budget_tax_bill_pension_ages: Retirement in 2026: women at 62y 6m (36y 10m of service), men at 64y 9m (39y 10m of service).
budget_tax_bill_pension_caption: Simplified: pension ≈ insurable income × 1.35% × years of service, bounded by the 2026 minimum (€322) and maximum (€1,738) pension. The real formula weighs lifetime earnings against the national average.
budget_tax_bill_caption: Approximate estimate. Assumes a default-case taxpayer (born after 1959, no second-pillar opt-out). SSC stops accruing above the МОД; above the cap only the 10% income tax keeps scaling. Social-security contributions are earmarked (pension + health + unemployment), not freely allocable — the breakdown applies general-government spending shares to the whole bill purely for illustration.
budget_tax_bill_company_caption: Simplified: 10% corporate tax on profit, then 5% tax when the remainder is distributed as a dividend. Corporate tax is assessed annually; the owner's own social-security contributions are not modelled here.
budget_tax_bill_curve_title: Effective rate across the salary range
budget_tax_bill_curve_caption: Effective tax-and-contribution rate by gross salary. Once salary passes the МОД cap social security stops growing and the rate falls — the burden turns regressive at the top.
budget_tax_bill_tip_ssc: Employee social-security and health contributions — pension 8.78%, health 3.20%, sickness/maternity 1.40%, unemployment 0.40%. Charged on insurable income up to the МОД cap.
budget_tax_bill_tip_mod: Maximum insurable income (МОД) — the monthly ceiling above which no further social-security contributions are due. Income tax still applies to the whole salary.
budget_tax_bill_tip_rate: Effective rate = total tax and contributions ÷ gross income. Marginal rate = the tax on the next euro earned; it falls to 10% above the МОД cap, where social security stops.
budget_tax_bill_tip_wedge: Tax wedge — the share of the full cost of employing you (gross salary plus employer contributions) taken by tax and social security rather than reaching your take-home pay.
budget_tax_calculator_page_description: Estimate your income tax, social-security contributions and VAT for an employee, self-employed person or company owner — and see which government functions your tax bill funds.
budget_tax_calculator_page_intro: Pick a taxpayer profile and income to see your monthly tax and social-security bill, the VAT embedded in your spending, and how it maps onto general-government spending — plus a projection of your future pension.
budget_citizen_tax_calc_link: What did your taxes buy? Open the tax calculator
budget_top_deviations_subtitle: Spending units whose execution diverged most from the original budget law, fiscal year
budget_top_deviations_caption: Plan = original ЗДБРБ appropriation. Executed = year-end execution report (Отчет за изпълнението на програмния бюджет). Only ministries with both figures and a planned budget over €20M are shown.
budget_journey_subtitle: Law → execution → audit, per fiscal year.
budget_methodology_description: Where the budget data comes from, how it is processed, and what the dashboard covers.
budget_meth_source_p: The figures come from the Ministry of Finance dataset “state budget execution by major budget indicators”, published on the national open-data portal data.egov.bg under a public-domain (CC0) licence. The Ministry publishes one resource per monthly cash-execution snapshot; each lists, for the five top-level sections of the state budget, the amount set by the budget law and the amount executed so far that fiscal year.
budget_meth_sections_p: The Bulgarian state budget execution table has five top-level sections, and the dashboard tracks each of them over time:
budget_meth_revenue: — tax and non-tax revenue, grants and donations.
budget_meth_expenditure: — spending and transfers: personnel, operations, capital, social spending, subsidies, interest.
budget_meth_eu: — Bulgaria's contribution to the common EU budget.
budget_meth_balance: — revenue minus expenditure minus the EU contribution. Negative means a deficit.
budget_meth_financing: — how the balance is financed: external and domestic borrowing, deposits, privatisation.
budget_meth_currency_p: Bulgaria adopted the euro on 1 January 2026. Monthly snapshots up to and including December 2025 are published in leva; from January 2026 they are in euro. Every figure on the dashboard is shown in euro.
budget_meth_cumulative_p: Each monthly snapshot is cumulative year-to-date: the December figure is the whole year, and the series resets each January. The execution-trend chart shows this directly — a ramp within each fiscal year, then a reset.
budget_meth_integrity_p: The ingest fails loudly rather than publish partial data: a pinned snapshot is re-parsed on every run and byte-compared against a committed fixture, so any drift in the parser is caught before anything is written, and a change touching more than a small fraction of the data aborts the run for review.
budget_meth_ministries_p: Each first-level spending unit (ministry, agency, independent body) publishes its own annual execution report. The ingest parses these PDFs into a uniform admin-grain table and reconciles them against the State Budget Law, so each ministry page shows planned versus actual at the ministry level and one column deeper at the program level.
budget_meth_documents_p: Each fiscal year is linked to its source documents — the State Budget Law as promulgated in Държавен вестник, the mid-year amendment laws, the year-end execution report, and the report of the National Audit Office. The fiscal-year page surfaces the timeline and the original links.
budget_meth_procurement_p: Each spending unit's page links to the public-procurement contracts it awarded under the Public Procurement Act, so a budget line can be followed all the way down to the individual contracts that consumed it.

annual_reports_intro: Political parties must file an annual financial report with the Court of Audit by 31 March each year (Political Parties Act, art. 34). Each row tracks one party's filing record — search for a party, or sort by compliance.
annual_reports_status_on_time_full: Filed by the 31 March deadline and meeting the form, content and submission requirements.
annual_reports_status_non_compliant_full: Filed on time but not meeting the form, content or submission requirements.
annual_reports_status_not_filed_full: No annual financial report filed with the Court of Audit.

chart_event_gfc_desc: Contraction of world trade and capital flows after the Lehman Brothers collapse (Sep 2008). For Bulgaria — sharp GDP fall and unemployment spike through 2009–2010.
chart_event_borisov1_protests_desc: Anti-monopoly and electricity-bill protests in Feb–Mar 2013 that brought down GERB's first cabinet four months before the regular election.
chart_event_dans_desc: Year-long civic mobilisation against the Oresharski cabinet, started days after Delyan Peevski was appointed head of DANS (June 2013).
chart_event_ktb_desc: Bank run and licence withdrawal of Corporate Commercial Bank in summer 2014 — Bulgaria's largest banking crisis since the 1990s. Hit the fiscal reserve and trust in the banking supervisor.
chart_event_covid_desc: Global pandemic from the WHO declaration (March 2020) to the lifting of Bulgaria's epidemic state of emergency (April 2022). Drove the 2020 GDP contraction and the 2021–2023 inflation cycle.
chart_event_borisov3_protests_desc: Anti-government and anti-corruption protests through summer and autumn 2020 — did not topple the cabinet but reshaped the 2021 election cycle.
chart_event_autumn_2025_protests_desc: Civic protests in autumn 2025, during the first year of the Zhelyazkov cabinet's term.

my_area_no_id: Pick a settlement or municipality to open the dashboard.
my_area_unknown_description: No settlement or municipality matched this identifier.
my_area_entry_description: Enter your settlement or municipality name to see a personalized dashboard: MPs, mayor and council, budget, EU funds, census and more.
my_area_entry_help: The service uses your location only once to find the closest settlement — nothing is stored, nothing is sent anywhere.
area_pill_tooltip: Your selected area. Click to open the dashboard, or × to clear.
about_my_area: The new "My Area" page consolidates everything about your chosen settlement or municipality: MPs for your MIR, the mayor and council, Art. 53 transfers, capital programmes, EU-funded projects, census, indicators and more — all in one place. Reached via the crosshair icon in the top bar or directly via /my-area. Additional layers (municipal transparency, schools, services, air quality, crime) appear automatically once their respective data is ingested.
my_area_iisda_mayors_source: iisda.government.bg — Mayors of municipalities (Административен регистър): email addresses for ~260 municipal mayors and ~600 deputy mayors, surfaced as inline mailto: icons next to each official's name on the My-Area government card. Scraped from detail pages in the ID range 4400–4950 — each page contributes one mayor block plus one block per Заместник-кмет — and matched to obshtina codes via municipalities.json.
my_area_lisi_source: Transparency International Bulgaria — Local Integrity System Index (LISI): 27 oblast-center municipalities scored across 9 institutional pillars (procurement, budget, council oversight, conflict of interest, citizen participation, audit, asset declarations, open data, integrity response), 0–5 scale. Powers the "Local government transparency" tile — 2024 national average 3.27, leaders Burgas 3.71 / Ruse 3.70 / Sofia 3.61.
my_area_iaos_air_source: ИАОС — air quality monitoring (data.egov.bg): quarterly per-pollutant CSVs for PM10 (47 stations) and PM2.5 (10 stations). Powers the "Air quality" tile; stations identified by name ("София — Хиподрума", "Пловдив — Каменица") and attached to municipalities via prefix parsing.
my_area_tax_receipt_explainer: See how much the state sends back to your municipality per resident, and enter your monthly gross salary to see how your personal income tax (10%) is allocated across the government's functional spending categories.
my_area_tax_receipt_prompt: Enter your monthly gross salary to see the allocation.
my_area_tax_receipt_municipal_return_not: State transfer envelope (delegated activities, equalization and targeted subsidies), {{year}} budget — funded from the whole tax mix, not just your income tax.
my_area_tax_receipt_disclaimer: The calculator counts personal income tax only (10%). VAT, social security contributions and corporate taxes are not in the sum. The allocation reflects the overall government budget mix, not earmarked spending.
my_area_tax_receipt_local_taxes_heading: Local taxes you pay to your municipality
my_area_tax_receipt_local_taxes_caveat: Estimates on a stylised household (apartment with €30,000 tax valuation + 85 kW sedan). Your actual bill depends on the property's tax valuation and the vehicle's power.
my_area_tax_receipt_tbo_council_decides: Council sets the rate annually — see the bylaw
my_area_tax_receipt_capital_programs_hea: {{year}} capital programme — top projects in your район
my_area_tax_receipt_calculator_cta: Full calculator: tax, social security & VAT
my_area_local_taxes_source: IME — Local taxes (265 общини): five indicators × every municipality × 2021–2025. Ranking is ascending by rate (#1 = lowest rate). Resident-facing fees (garbage, tourist, dog) are parsed from per-municipality bylaws and merged into the same record.
my_area_schools_caveat: Average of latest НВО (7th grade) + ДЗИ (matura) scores from МОН.
my_area_air_caveat: Readings from ИАОС + EEA stations. EU thresholds are colour-coded.
my_area_projects_map_caveat: Showing {{shown}} of {{total}} contracts. Marker size reflects amount; colour reflects status.
my_area_projects_list_caveat: Showing the top {{shown}} of {{total}} contracts by amount.
my_area_alerts_caveat: Chronological digest of council decisions, procurement awards, EU-funded projects, capital programmes, and parliamentary votes that mention this municipality. Not exhaustive.
my_area_council_ai_disclaimer: Summaries are AI-generated. For the full text, follow the link to the original resolution.
